cases.description: Mapped from execution-specs CREATE zero-byte create benchmark onto a single wrapper that stores create success, created address, and created code size.
notes: Upstream intent: benchmark CREATE with zero-byte initcode and value 1.

RPC mapping: a single funded deployed wrapper performs one value 1 CREATE, then stores create success, the returned child address, EXTCODESIZE(child) and child balance as deterministic witness fields.
Only zero-byte variants are admitted; non-empty/max-code-size variants remain blocked until their code-size witness contracts are mapped.
Admitted because final receipt status plus wrapper-exposed create success, nonzero child address, zero child code size and child balance are deterministic final observables.

cases.description: Mapped from execution-specs CREATE zero-byte create benchmark onto a single wrapper that stores create success, created address, and created code size.
notes: Upstream intent: benchmark CREATE with zero-byte initcode and zero value.
RPC mapping: a single funded deployed wrapper performs one zero value CREATE, then stores create success, the returned child address, EXTCODESIZE(child) as deterministic witness fields.
Only zero-byte variants are admitted; non-empty/max-code-size variants remain blocked until their code-size witness contracts are mapped.
Admitted because final receipt status plus wrapper-exposed create success, nonzero child address, zero child code size are deterministic final observables.

cases.description: Mapped from execution-specs CREATE non-empty non-zero data create benchmark onto a single wrapper that stores create success, created address, created code size, and created code hash.
cases.upstream_ref: tests/benchmark/compute/instruction/test_system.py::test_create[opcode=CREATE-variant=0.25x max code size with non-zero data]
notes: Upstream intent: benchmark CREATE with 6144-byte non-zero initcode that deploys 6144-byte child code.

RPC mapping: a single deployed wrapper performs one zero value CREATE, then stores create success, the returned child address, EXTCODESIZE(child), and EXTCODEHASH(child) as deterministic witness fields.
Only the smallest non-zero-data and zero-data non-empty variants are admitted; larger code-size ratios and value-bearing non-empty variants remain blocked until separate witness contracts are mapped.
Admitted because final receipt status plus wrapper-exposed create success, nonzero child address, child code size, and child code hash are deterministic final observables.

notes: Upstream intent: benchmark CREATE with 6144-byte zero initcode that deploys 6144-byte child code.

RPC mapping: a single deployed wrapper performs one zero value CREATE, then stores create success, the returned child address, EXTCODESIZE(child), and EXTCODEHASH(child) as deterministic witness fields.
Only the smallest non-zero-data and zero-data non-empty variants are admitted; larger code-size ratios and value-bearing non-empty variants remain blocked until separate witness contracts are mapped.
Admitted because final receipt status plus wrapper-exposed create success, nonzero child address, child code size, and child code hash are deterministic final observables.

cases.upstream_ref: tests/benchmark/compute/instruction/test_system.py::test_create[opcode=CREATE2-variant=0.25x max code size with zero data]
notes: Upstream intent: benchmark CREATE2 with 6144-byte zero initcode that deploys 6144-byte child code.

cases.description: Mapped from execution-specs RETURN benchmark onto a self-call wrapper that stores inner-call success, returndata size, and returndata digest for the 1024-byte non-zero variant.
notes: Upstream intent: benchmark RETURN with a 1024-byte non-zero returndata payload.
RPC mapping: a single deployed contract self-CALLs into its child path so the outer wrapper can observe the inner result without requiring multi-contract orchestration in the manifest.
The child path fills the first 1024 memory byte(s) with 0xff before RETURN.

Wrapper witness contract stores the inner-call success bit in slot0, RETURNDATASIZE in slot1, and KECCAK256(returndata) in slot2; the outer transaction receipt remains successful so receipt_status stays directly observable.
Admitted because final receipt status plus wrapper-exposed returndata digest/size are deterministic final observables for the success path.

cases.description: Mapped from execution-specs RETURN benchmark onto a self-call wrapper that stores inner-call success, returndata size, and returndata digest for the 1024-byte zero variant.
notes: Upstream intent: benchmark RETURN with a 1024-byte zero returndata payload.
RPC mapping: a single deployed contract self-CALLs into its child path so the outer wrapper can observe the inner result without requiring multi-contract orchestration in the manifest.
The child path RETURNs 1024 byte(s) from zero-initialized memory.

Wrapper witness contract stores the inner-call success bit in slot0, RETURNDATASIZE in slot1, and KECCAK256(returndata) in slot2; the outer transaction receipt remains successful so receipt_status stays directly observable.
Admitted because final receipt status plus wrapper-exposed returndata digest/size are deterministic final observables for the success path.
